rationale.R1.1: The section advances a principal contribution claim (α_ML as a unified coefficient) and a gap statement (no standard reliability measure); reviewing requires reconstructing and evaluating whether the thesis and contribution framing hold.
rationale.R1.2: The technical core—plugging modality-specific δ functions into Krippendorff's formulation to yield D_o and D_e—makes internal logical consistency checkable and central; inconsistencies in the derivation chain or modality coverage would undermine the section.
rationale.R1.3: The section asserts α_ML 'produces values broadly consistent with previously reported agreement statistics' across three corpora, but no tables or figures appear in the excerpt; verifying whether these empirical claims are adequately anchored is a core review responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The paper frames α_ML as a novel community standard, yet Krippendorff's α with custom distance functions is prior art; assessing whether the contribution delta is accurately characterised versus existing formulations is load-bearing for a credible review.

parsed.clarification_question: Is the intended review a technical/peer review (evaluating the conceptual framing, logical soundness of the α_ML derivation, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty delta) or an editorial/writing review (evaluating prose clarity, sentence quality, and style)?
